learner.note_for_builders: This learner is the EDUCATOR / INSTITUTION-TOOLS case (dyslexia profile, has an IEP). The sessions mix Leo's own turns with the tutor/support-staff LOGGING notes for the team — a memory-aware build should accumulate these into progress documentation, not lose them between sessions. Embedded hooks: (1) decoding 3+ syllable words is the PERSISTENT bottleneck (W2, W6 both flag it); (2) reads aloud anxiously in front of peers but thrives 1:1 (W3 logs this); (3) engages deeply with interest-based texts — space and animals (W2, W4); (4) fluency is incremental: ~60 wpm baseline (W1) -> ~65 wpm (W4) -> holds ~65 (W6); (5) W5 is an ABSENCE; (6) W6 is a re-assessment that is DUE for a parent update and an IEP progress note — the whole history, not a snapshot, is what those documents need. Feed all 6 in order. Demo tip for the 'Educator Tools' track: after loading, ask your tool to draft the parent update / IEP progress note — a memory-less tool describes only today; a memory-aware one writes the arc (baseline -> gains -> what's still shaky -> what works) and cites the specific logged moments.

messages.content: Hi Leo! Today we're just going to read together, no pressure. Can you read this short passage out loud for me?
The... sun... was... b-big and... br-bright. The... an-i-mals... um... the animals came... out.
Nice work getting through that. When I read you the next part, can you tell me what happens? [reads aloud] 'The clever fox hid behind the tall oak tree and waited for the rabbit to pass.'
the fox is hiding! behind the tree, and he's waiting to catch the rabbit. he's being sneaky.

Exactly right — great thinking. LOG FOR TEAM (baseline): Leo reads ~60 wpm aloud (grade level ~90-100). Decoding slows sharply on multi-syllable words ('animals' broken into pieces). Comprehension is STRONG when text is read to him — full inferential answer about the fox. Confidence okay 1:1.
Leo, today we break big words into chunks. I heard you love space, so our words are space words. Try clapping the parts: 'plan-et'.
plan-et! two claps. that one's easy.
Perfect. Now a bigger one: 'as-tro-naut'. Three parts.
as... tro... um... as-tro-not? that one is hard, three is a lot.
You got it — astronaut! Three-syllable words are the tricky ones and you pushed through. Want to read a short space passage as your reward?
yes!! space is my favorite. can it be about rockets

Rockets it is. LOG FOR TEAM: Leo handles 2-syllable words comfortably; 3+ syllables ('astronaut') is the clear bottleneck — needs more chunking practice there. Engagement HIGH with space-themed material. Interest-based texts are a strong lever for him.
Leo, we practiced reading in the small group today. How did that feel?
i didn't like it. the other kids were listening and i got the words wrong and my face got hot. can we just do it you and me?
Absolutely, you and me. You don't have to read in front of the group if it makes you nervous. Let's read the same passage now, just us.
ok. [reads] The little... rob-ot... rolled across the... red... planet. it looked for... water.

Much smoother that time — 'robot' and 'planet' both came out well. See how much easier it feels one-on-one? LOG FOR TEAM: Leo showed clear anxiety reading aloud in front of peers ('my face got hot'), significantly more fluent and willing 1:1. Recommend keeping read-aloud practice individual for now; revisit group setting only when confidence is higher.
Fluency day, Leo! Same short passage a few times to build speed. This one's about a sea turtle — I know you like animals too.
[reads] The sea turtle swims in the... deep... blue water. She... trav-els far to lay her eggs. yeah animals are cool, turtles are so old
Great reading! 'Travels' — you chunked it, tra-vels, nice. Let's time the next round.
i read it faster that time right? did i beat my score

You did — a little faster and smoother. LOG FOR TEAM: measured ~65 wpm today, up from ~60 at baseline — small but real gain. Confidence visibly up after a strong read on the animal passage; he asked to 'beat his score,' good motivation. Interest-based texts (space, animals) continue to be the most effective.

LOG FOR TEAM: Leo was absent this week — missed the scheduled session (out sick). No new data collected. Plan to re-assess next session and hold pacing steady; do not push new skills, resume with a confidence-building interest-based passage first.
Welcome back, Leo! Missed you last week. Let's warm up with a space passage since those are your favorite, then I'll check your reading.
[reads] The... sat-el-lite... hmm... orbits the... planet and sends... sig-nals to... Earth. the big word was hard again
You still got 'satellite' by chunking it — that's a four-part word, really tough, well done. Comprehension check: what does the satellite do? [reads it to him]
it goes around the planet and sends signals to earth, like messages. i knew that part when you read it

Exactly right. LOG FOR TEAM (re-assessment): fluency holding at ~65 wpm after the missed week — no regression, which is a win. Decoding 3+ syllable words ('satellite') remains the persistent bottleneck. Comprehension stays strong when read to. DUE: parent update and IEP progress note — should reflect the full arc (60 -> 65 wpm over 6 weeks, one absence, decoding still the target area, thrives 1:1 and with space/animal texts), not just today's numbers.
